seo.title: Michigan Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in Michigan — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license michigan, dental assistant certification michigan, RDA michigan, DANB CDA michigan, dental assistant radiography michigan

license_types.title: Registered Dental Assistant (RDA)
license_types.description: The primary credential for dental assistants in Michigan.

steps.title: Meet the Eligibility Requirements
steps.description: Graduate from a CODA-accredited dental assisting program or complete the required on-the-job training under a licensed dentist.

steps.title: Obtain CPR Certification
steps.description: Hold a current certification in Basic Life Support (BLS) or Cardiopulmonary Resuscitation (CPR).

steps.title: Submit an Application
steps.description: Apply for registration with the Michigan Board of Dentistry and pay the required fee.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Dental assistants can apply for Registered Dental Assistant (RDA) through the Michigan Board of Dentistry by registration.

faqs.question: Does Michigan require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: Yes, Michigan requires Registered Dental Assistant (RDA) through the state board.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Michigan?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Michigan.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Michigan?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Michigan?
faqs.answer: State application fees are $50. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

reciprocity.notes: Dental assistants can apply for Registered Dental Assistant (RDA) through the Michigan Board of Dentistry by registration.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in Michigan
hero.dek: Michigan Dental Assistant registration requires graduation from a CODA-accredited dental assisting program or completion of required training, a CPR certification, and completion of required continuing education.
